nodes.name: 湿地排水
nodes.summary: 湿地被排干转为农业或建设用地。

nodes.name: 鹤形目
nodes.summary: 鹤形目（Gruiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联126种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、灰冠鹤、Grey-winged trumpeter等126种鸟类。

nodes.name: 灰鹤
nodes.summary: 灰鹤（Grus grus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如灰鹤。

nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 鹤属
nodes.summary: 鹤属（Grus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联9种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、沙丘鹤、肉垂鹤等9种鸟类。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

nodes.name: 鹤科
nodes.summary: 鹤科（Gruidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联11种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、灰冠鹤、黑冕鶴等11种鸟类。

nodes.name: 农药污染
nodes.summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 灰鹤
nodes.summary: 灰鹤（Common crane，学名 Grus grus），分类上属于鹤形目，并归入鹤科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，常见栖息地包括湿地，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、湿地排水、农药污染等4项。
nodes.shortSummary: 灰鹤（学名：Grus grus），别名漆鶴、千岁鹤或歐亞鶴，是鹤科鹤属的一种，是分布範圍較廣而較常見的鶴，主要分布於古北界地區。 辨識特征 就體型來說，灰鶴是中至大型的鶴類，身長 100 至 130 公分（翼展可達甚至超過 2 公尺）；高 100 至 130 公分；體重 3 至…

nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Wetlands are preferred habitats for the cranes.]] In Europe, the common crane predominantly breeds in boreal and taiga forest and mixed forests, from an elevation of sea-level to

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The Great Crane Project|url=http://www.thegreatcraneproject.org.uk/project/where-are-we-now|access-date=2021-02-06|website=www.thegreatcraneproject.org.uk}} In 2021, the British population had increased to 72 pairs. In the Republic of Ireland, several visiting flocks were observed in the 2000s and in 2021 a pair managed to breed for the first time on the island in 300 years. The main threat to the species and the primary reason for its decline comes from habitat loss and degradation, as a result of dam construction, urbanisation, agricultural expansion, and drainage of wetlands

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The Great Crane Project|url=http://www.thegreatcraneproject.org.uk/project/where-are-we-now|access-date=2021-02-06|website=www.thegreatcraneproject.org.uk}} In 2021, the British population had increased to 72 pairs. In the Republic of Ireland, several visiting flocks were observed in the 2000s and in 2021 a pair managed to breed for the first time on the island in 300 years. The main threat to the species and the primary reason for its decline comes from habitat loss and degradation, as a result of dam construction, urbanisation, agricultural expansion, and drainage of wetlands

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Further threats may include persecution due to crop damage, pesticide poisoning, egg collection, and hunting. The common crane is one of the species to which the Agreement on the Conservation of African-Eurasian Migratory Waterbirds (AEWA) applies
